skill: Systems of two linear equations in two variables
stem: A group of 252 campers went on a weekend trip and used a total of 80 cabins. Some of the cabins held 3 people each, and the rest held 4 people each. Assuming all the cabins were filled to capacity and every camper got to sleep in a cabin, exactly how many of the cabins were 3-person cabins?
rationale: Let x be the number of 3-person cabins and y the number of 4-person cabins. Then x + y = 80 and 3x + 4y = 252. From the first equation, y = 80 − x. Substituting: 3x + 4(80 − x) = 252, which gives 320 − x = 252, so x = 68. The correct answer is choice B.
